doaObservations.finding: The delegated-org picker is now CONFIRMED rendered by five further DoA traces beyond the CSV trace the base spec cited (9a918b86). Every trace renders the SAME three radio options as org-name (<strong>) + full postal address, in the same order: 'IPAFFS Plant Agency F — 22 Acacia Avenue, London, N17 8LR'; 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1 — KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT'; 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2 — KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT'. IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1 is the option selected in each. This is the same agent fixture (Carol Clark / b2cDoaAgent) across all traces, so the list is stable per-agent.
traces: b1f31ed70fbdb2f92f66488da5bda6f3e510a0c9 action 13 (before-snapshot); option checked at action 12

doaObservations.finding: DOWNSTREAM CONFIRMED — the org chosen here is the notification's owning tenant and seeds later pages. It is now CONFIRMED rendered (not just asserted by test) that the selected delegated org auto-populates: the Importer on the Traders/Addresses page (trace 2c16ad65 action 77), the Consignee on the dashboard result card (traces e9b5e36b, 065de8c5, 545412266), and the responsible-person Contact block on the review page (trace 545412266 action 95). Values for IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1: importer 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1 / KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6, UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT'; contact 'Isabel Irwin / ipaffs-plant-org-1-isabel@mailinator.com / 0123456789'. See traders-addresses.json, contact-details.json and authorization-rules.md.

outOfScopeControls.note: A second <form> exists on the page but is OUTSIDE <main> — the footer cookies form (action=/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/cookies, method=post) carrying its own crumb plus hidden returnUrl and fromFooterHeader inputs. It is chrome, not part of this question, and is deliberately excluded from `fields`. Recorded here so a later reader does not mistake it for a missed control.
outOfScopeControls.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 — INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED: eval over document.querySelectorAll('input,select,textarea') returns exactly 8 controls — inMain=true: crumb(hidden), notification-type(hidden, CHEDPP), organisation/organisation-2/organisation-3 (radio); inMain=false: crumb(hidden), returnUrl(hidden), fromFooterHeader(hidden). Zero selects, zero textareas. The `fields` list accounts for every in-main control that is not CSRF or type plumbing — no missed controls.

payloadContract.note: What this page actually POSTs, and what the server will accept. Established by re-derivation; not present in the previous revision. The form submits `crumb` + `notification-type` + `organisation` and NOTHING ELSE — critically, the submit button carries NO name attribute (only id/value/type), so its value 'Save and continue' is never sent. That matters because the validator ALSO composes SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA, which permits three OPTIONAL save-action keys (save-and-continue / save-and-return / save-and-review) that this page's markup never actually submits. So the accepted payload is wider than the rendered form. The rebuild should not inherit the optional save-action keys unless it renders named submit buttons.
payloadContract.evidence: Button outerHTML has no name attribute (trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24, verbatim above); control inventory at action 24 (8 inputs, listed under outOfScopeControls); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:11-22 (schema = organisation + notification-type + ...SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA) and :24-29 (ETAG_SCHEMA folded in only when requireEtag); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:30-43 (SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA = save-and-continue + save-and-return + save-and-review, each .optional()); validation runs with { abortEarly: false } (validator:31), so multiple field errors can surface at once.

citationNote: READ THIS BEFORE FOLLOWING ANY CITATION. Three DIFFERENT files in the legacy repo share the basename `consignment_organisation.js`, and a previous revision of this spec cited them by bare basename — which is genuinely ambiguous and, worse, actively misleading, because the same revision separately discussed the client bundle as unread mystery behaviour while citing the route handler by the identical name. All citations in this revision are repo-relative and unambiguous. The three files, all under ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/: (1) assets/javascripts/consignment_organisation.js — the CLIENT bundle served as /public/javascripts/consignment_organisation.js (the accessible-autocomplete enhancement; 20 lines); (2) routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js — the hapi ROUTE HANDLER (GET/POST, view context, sorting, overdue-debt); (3) validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js — the Joi VALIDATOR (the rules and error messages). There is also a fourth, routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation_change.js, for the amend/change variant.

govukComponents.usage: One radio per organisation the signed-in agent may act for. Name=organisation, value=organisation GUID. Rendered only when organisations.length < 8; at 8 or more the template renders a govuk select instead. The tests address these radios purely by accessible name — page.getByRole('radio', { name: companyName }) — so the organisation NAME is the de-facto contract the tests rely on.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 (rendered HTML: 3x div.govuk-radios__item; eval: radios=3, selects=0; action 24's own source location is OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:39:42, i.e. the radioCompany check inside selectDifferentOrganisation); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentOrganisation.html:22-39; corroborated by /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:14-16 (radioCompany(companyName) => getByRole('radio', { name: companyName }))

govukComponents.usage: Primary submit. OBSERVED verbatim outerHTML (as re-read from the trace DOM — note data-govuk-button-init="", which govuk-frontend's own JS stamps on at init and which a previous revision of this spec omitted while claiming the quote was verbatim): <button class="govuk-button" id="button-save-and-continue" type="submit" value="Save and continue" data-module="govuk-button" data-prevent-double-click="true" data-govuk-button-init="">Save and continue</button>. The server-rendered form of it (template) carries no data-govuk-button-init. The button has NO name attribute, so its value is never submitted — see payloadContract. A 'Save and review' variant (id=button-save-and-review) renders instead when showSaveAndReviewButton is true — INFERRED from the template and from the page object, never observed rendering. Both buttons are located by the tests via getByRole('button', { name: ... }) — i.e. by visible text, not by id.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 (outerHTML above, verbatim incl. data-govuk-button-init); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentOrganisation.html:45-51; OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:10-12 (btnSaveAndContinue) and :22-24 (btnSaveAndReview)

Which company is this notification for

govukComponents.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentOrganisation.html:12; ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:46-50 (viewContext.errors = errorHelper.mapErrors(validationResult), then re-render of the same view — note it re-renders rather than redirects, so errors survive without PRG); absence verified in trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 and corroborated across /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/tests (no assertion on any organisation error message)

govukComponents.usage: ALTERNATIVE control. When the agent holds 8 or more delegations the template swaps the radio group for a govuk select with a visually hidden label (label text = the H1) and a topOption of 'Select organisation'. IMPORTANT ADDITION: that select does not stay a plain select — the page's client script progressively enhances it into a GDS accessible autocomplete (with 'No Importer found' as the no-results text), UNLESS one of the options is disabled (an overdue-debt org), in which case the enhancement is skipped and the user gets a bare select. So the 8+ path is really a THIRD control variant, and it silently degrades to a FOURTH. Never exercised in any trace (verified: zero <select> elements in main — the observed agent had 3 organisations). Also unreachable via the tests: the page object exposes ONLY radio locators and no select/dropdown locator, so if a test environment ever gave an agent 8+ delegations the whole DoA suite would break. Untested in both sources.
govukComponents.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentOrganisation.html:29-39; ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/assets/javascripts/consignment_organisation.js:4-19 (enhanceSelectElement + the disabled-option guard); OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:6-24 (radio-only locators, no dropdown getter — contrast e.g. CountryOfOriginPage's dropdownCountryOfOrigin, used at ched-pp-workflows.ts:268)

govukComponents.usage: Overdue-debt warning (importer/overdueDebt/overdueDebtWarningDoA), shown when ANY delegated organisation is flagged as an overdue debtor — note the flag is computed across the whole delegation set (Object.values(overdueDebtors).some(...)), so one bad org warns on the whole page, not per-option. CORRECTION to a previous revision: the partial has now been READ from the authorised source and it is NOT a govuk-warning-text component at all — on this (non-review) page it renders a collapsible <details class="govuk-details"> with summary 'Why some options are not available', not a govuk-warning-text banner (the review-page branch, isReviewPage, instead renders a govuk-error-summary titled 'There is a problem'). VERBATIM copy (legacy): body heading 'There is a problem'; para 1 'You cannot submit a CHEDPP import notification on behalf of {commaSeparated overdueDebtorOrgNames}.' (the word 'submit' becomes 'clone' in the clone flow, {{#if cloning}}); para 2 'Please ask {commaSeparated overdueDebtorOrgNames} to contact us on 01633 631800 to discuss the reactivation of their account.' with the phone number linked as <a href="tel:01633631800">. Still never observed rendering (verified: zero .govuk-warning-text elements) and no test fixture creates an overdue-debt organisation, so this is untested in both sources — but its copy is no longer a GAP.
govukComponents.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentOrganisation.html:42-44; overdue-debt copy verbatim at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/partials/importer/overdueDebt/overdueDebtWarningDoA.html:1-31 (legacy — details summary :13-16, 'There is a problem' :5 and :20, para 1 :24, para 2 :25); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:57-58 and :69 (areMultipleUsersOverdueDebtors, shouldShowOverdueDebt = some(value === true)); no overdue-debt user/org in /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/fixtures/index.ts

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: <div class="govuk-radios"> inside <div class="govuk-form-group"> with NO <fieldset> and NO <legend>
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the three organisation radios as a bare group. The H1 sits outside the group as a plain <h1 class="govuk-heading-xl">, so nothing programmatically associates the question with the radios.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is the GOV.UK Radios component used in a non-standard and inaccessible way. The Design System requires radios to be wrapped in a fieldset whose legend is the question — screen-reader users otherwise hear three unlabelled radios with no question context. Verified by eval at action 24: fieldsets=0, legends=0, radios=3. AGGRAVATING FACTOR from source 2, now traced to its ROOT CAUSE: the CHED-PP accessibility suite pins itself to a NON-DoA user (test.use({ storageState: AUTH_FILES.b2cNotifier }) at ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:16), and a non-DoA user has no delegations, so this page is not merely skipped by that suite — it is STRUCTURALLY UNREACHABLE from it. The suite drives b2cNotifier from the Import Type Page (:23-28) straight to the Country of Origin Page (:30-33). No axe scan has ever run against this markup, and none can until the suite gains a DoA-agent scan. The rebuild must NOT copy this: wrap the radios in govuk-fieldset with the H1 as govuk-fieldset__legend (govuk-fieldset__legend--xl, heading-inside-legend), and add a DoA-agent pass to the a11y suite so the organisation page is actually scanned.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Radios with Fieldset — legend containing the h1 (the standard 'one question per page' radios pattern).
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 (eval over main: fieldsets=0 legends=0 radios=3 selects=0); consignmentOrganisation.html:22-28; a11y coverage gap at /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:16 (storageState b2cNotifier — non-DoA) and :23-32 (Import Type Page -> Country of Origin Page, org page absent)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern:
Rich HTML inside govuk-radios__label — Org name
address line
city
postcode
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Packs the organisation name AND its full address into the radio's
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The label becomes a multi-line address block, so the accessible name of each radio is the whole run-together address. Verified verbatim from the a11y tree: radio "IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1 KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT BELFAST BT7 1NT". GDS guidance is that the label is the short choice and supporting detail belongs in a radio hint. CORROBORATED as a real cost in source 2: the page object can only target these radios by a SUBSTRING of that accessible name (getByRole('radio', { name: companyName }) with companyName='IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1'), which relies on Playwright's substring matching and would break — or match ambiguously — the moment two delegated orgs share a name prefix. Note the traced data already contains a near-miss: 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' is a strict prefix of nothing here, but 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2' shares the whole prefix up to the final digit. In the rebuild: label = organisation name, address = govuk-radios__hint / govuk-hint.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Radios with hint text on each item (govuk-radios__hint), or the Radios 'divider'/hint pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence:
trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 (a11y snapshot accessible names; form-group outerHTML shows …
markup); OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:14-16

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Control-type switch at a threshold of 8 — {{#lt organisations.length 8}} radios {{else}} select {{/lt}}
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders a radio group for fewer than 8 organisations and a <select> (with a visually hidden label and a 'Select organisation' top option) for 8 or more. The select is then JS-enhanced into an accessible autocomplete unless an option is disabled — so the branch really yields THREE rendered outcomes, not two.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A bespoke adaptive-control rule with a magic number: the same question renders as different components depending on data volume — multiple code paths, multiple validation/error-display behaviours, multiple test surfaces. NUANCE vs a previous revision, which framed this as IPAFFS reaching for a plain Select against GDS advice: IPAFFS does in fact reach for the accessible autocomplete on the 8+ path, which is the GDS-sanctioned answer for long option lists. The real defect is therefore not the choice of control but the BRANCHING itself — a silent, data-dependent switch between three renderings, only one of which (radios) is ever traced or tested, and whose autocomplete quietly disappears when an org is an overdue debtor. The threshold of 8 has no traced justification. NEVER exercised by any trace AND structurally untestable by the current suite (the page object has radio locators only), so the select and autocomplete paths are unverified end-to-end. The rebuild should pick ONE control and apply it unconditionally.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Radios throughout, or the accessible-autocomplete pattern applied consistently — not a data-volume branch with a disabled-option escape hatch.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Disabled option carrying its reason inside the option text — text: organisationName + ', ' + address + ' (This option is not available)' with disabled: true and hint: { text: 'This option is not available' }
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: For an organisation flagged as an overdue debtor, the option is disabled and the string ' (This option is not available)' is appended into the option's own text, in addition to a hint carrying the same words.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Duplicates the same message into both the label text and the hint, and relies on a disabled control to communicate a business rule. Disabled form controls are a known GDS anti-pattern (poor contrast, not focusable, no explanation to screen-reader users). Never observed rendering — the traced agent had no overdue-debt orgs — and no test fixture creates one. The rebuild should either omit ineligible orgs with an explanation, or keep them selectable and fail validation with a real error message.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Radio hint text for the reason, or omit the option and explain via inset text / error message — not a disabled control.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence:
ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:73-83 (getViewContextOrganisation — text with the ' (This option is not available)' suffix, disabled: overdueDebtor, hint carrying the same words). NOTE, newly established: this `text`/`disabled`/`hint` shape is the SELECT variant's option model. The radio variant does not render `text` at all — the observed radio labels are composed by the organisationRadioGroup partial as name
address, with no comma after the name, whereas `text` is name + ', ' + address. So `text` is used for SORTING (handler:60) and for the select's options, and the disabled/hint fields plausibly only bite on the select path. How the radio partial expresses a disabled org is UNVERIFIED — the partial could not be read (see openQuestions).

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Back inside
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A JS-driven back link rendered above main by the common/backLink partial. CORRECTION (verified from the rendered anchor's verbatim outerHTML): the link DOES use the Design System's govuk-back-link class — it is not a hand-rolled replacement. What is bespoke is (a) the custom MODIFIER back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs on the anchor itself, (b) the .navigation-links / .navigation-links__left-block wrapper divs, and (c) the href.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: href="#" means the link does nothing without JavaScript and cannot be opened in a new tab, middle-clicked or crawled — it degrades badly. The custom modifier and wrapper classes are IPAFFS-specific CSS outside the govuk-frontend toolbox, kept only to position the link relative to a breadcrumbs bar. The rebuild should use the standard Back link component with a real server-rendered href to the previous page in the journey, positioned via the standard page template rather than a custom modifier.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Back link (govuk-back-link) with a genuine URL and no custom modifier.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 — verbatim outerHTML via eval over anchors whose innerText is 'Back': Back. Also a11y snapshot: link "Back" [ref=e48] with /url: "#", rendered outside main. consignmentOrganisation.html:2-7

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A page-specific client-side JavaScript bundle loaded at the foot of the template. CONFIRMED to load at runtime: the trace's network log shows GET consignment_organisation.js?v=1.0.4005 -> 200, 1.5K. CORRECTION — a previous revision recorded its behaviour as unread and unknowable ('its 1.5K of behaviour was not read'). It HAS now been read, and it is 18 lines: on document ready it takes $('#organisation'), and if that element exists AND has no disabled <option>, it calls accessibleAutocomplete.enhanceSelectElement() on it — i.e. it progressively enhances the SELECT variant into a GDS accessible autocomplete (tNoResults 'No Importer found', minLength 2, showAllValues true, confirmOnBlur false). The disabled-option guard means an agent with an overdue-debt org silently gets a plain select with no autocomplete.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IT IS BROKEN ON THE OBSERVED (RADIO) PATH, AND THE TRACE PROVES IT. In the radio variant there is no <select>, but the FIRST RADIO's id is literally `organisation` (verified at action 24: input#organisation, type=radio) — so $('#organisation') still matches, .find('option:disabled') is empty on a radio so the guard passes, and the script hands a RADIO INPUT to enhanceSelectElement() as though it were a select. It throws. CONFIRMED in this trace's console at 0:17.553 — 'jQuery.Deferred exception TypeError: Array.prototype.filter called on null or undefined at z.enhanceSelectElement (accessible-autocomplete.min.js) at HTMLDocument.<anonymous> (consignment_organisation.js:10:30)' followed at 0:17.572 by the uncaught 'Array.prototype.filter called on null or undefined'. The timing pins it to THIS page: action 24 is at 0:17.395 and action 25 at 0:17.673, so the throw falls between them, and consignment_organisation.js loads on no other page. It is harmless only by luck — the exception escapes the ready handler and the radios, being plain HTML, still submit. So every DoA agent with fewer than 8 delegations loads 1.5K of JS whose only effect is a swallowed TypeError. This is dead-and-broken code and a strong argument for the id collision being a real defect: `organisation` names both the select AND the first radio. The rebuild must not port the script; if it adopts an autocomplete it must be for a real select and must not collide ids with a radio.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None needed — a progressively-enhanced radios + submit form requires no page-specific JS. If the rebuild keeps a select for long lists, use the GDS accessible autocomplete deliberately and consistently, not behind a length branch plus a disabled-option guard.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada network request 901 (GET 200, consignment_organisation.js?v=1.0.4005, 1.5K); console error at 0:17.553 and 0:17.572 with the stack naming consignment_organisation.js:10:30 (via `playwright trace console`); action 24 (input#organisation is a radio, radios=3 selects=0); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/assets/javascripts/consignment_organisation.js:1-20 (the whole file — the client bundle; NOT to be confused with the route handler or validator of the same basename, see citationNote); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentOrganisation.html:56

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: One question split across two pages — 'consignment-for' (own org vs 'A different organisation') then 'consignment-organisation' (which company)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Selecting the agent's OWN organisation is a single radio + Save and continue on the PREVIOUS page (consignment-for, H1 'Who are you creating this notification for?', options 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' and 'A different organisation'), while selecting a delegated organisation costs a second page — check 'A different organisation', continue, then pick the company here and continue again.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The user is answering one question — 'which company is this notification for?' — across two screens, and the agent's own organisation is privileged into a different control on a different page from every other organisation they can act for. CONFIRMED end-to-end by the trace: action 20 (import type) -> action 22 checks radio 'A different organisation' on consignment-for -> action 23 Save and continue -> action 24 checks 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' on THIS page -> action 25 Save and continue. Sequence also matches the page object's own methods: selectOwnOrganisation() is ONE check+click; selectDifferentOrganisation() is TWO. The split is visible in the QA repo as a modelling smell — ONE page object (OrganisationSelectionPage) owns locators for BOTH pages (radioDifferentOrganisation and radioOwnOrganisation at :6-8 and :18-20 belong to consignment-for; radioCompany at :14-16 belongs to this page), because the two screens are one question the tests cannot cleanly separate. GDS 'one thing per page' does not mean splitting one list of options by which option is special. The rebuild should consider a SINGLE page listing every organisation the user may act for (their own included) as one radio group — which also removes this page's conditional existence entirely.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A single Radios question listing all selectable organisations, own org included, with a fieldset legend as the H1.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada actions 20-25; snapshot at action 22 (main innerText of consignment-for, verbatim: 'About the consignment / Who are you creating this notification for? / IPAFFS Plant Agency C / A different organisation / Save and continue'); OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:6-41 (one page object spanning both pages); workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:259-264

fields.name: organisation
fields.label: Which company is this notification for
fields.requiredNote: required=true is `legacy` (read from authorised source, re-verified this pass) AND corroborated by the rendered DOM. The rule is explicit: `organisation: Joi.string().required().empty('').valid(...organisationIds)` at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:12 — required, empty string coerced to missing, value constrained to the signed-in agent's own delegation GUIDs (passed in from the handler at routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:44). Corroborated by the rendered DOM (no radio carries the checked attribute — verified at action 24); what remains unobserved is only the ERROR RENDERING, not the existence of the rule. POLICY NOTE: this mandatoriness is enforced ONLY by the frontend Joi validator — the `organisation` selection is a per-user agent-delegation choice, NOT a field in the ipaffs-imports-notification-schema PartOne model, so there is no @NotNull group annotation to cite for it. In the rebuild the selected organisation is the notification's owning tenant (see downstream findings), so 'required' will likely be expressed by the authorisation/tenancy model rather than as a nullable notification field — a deliberate rebuild decision, not a straight port. See validationMessages.
options: IPAFFS Plant Agency F 22 Acacia Avenue London N17 8LR
IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1 KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT BELFAST BT7 1NT
IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2 KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT BELFAST BT7 1NT
fields.optionsNote: These are the verbatim ACCESSIBLE NAMES of the three radios as rendered for this one agent (Carol Clark / b2cDoaAgent). They are per-user data, NOT reference data — a different agent sees a different list. The agent's OWN organisation ('IPAFFS Plant Agency C') is NOT among them; it is offered on the previous page instead. Option ORDER is alphabetical, case-insensitive, by the composed text.
fields.observedValuesNote: The submitted value is the organisation GUID, not the name. eb0ca011-bc72-eb11-a812-000d3a274b26 = 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' (id=organisation-2). The other two rendered GUIDs were fee8faa4-3b99-eb11-b1ac-000d3aaeef6b (Agency F, id=organisation) and 0e96ec84-c672-eb11-a812-000d3a22983f (Organisation 2, id=organisation-3); neither was ever selected by any test.
fields.conditionalOn: DoA agent only. The whole page is reached only after selecting 'A different organisation' on consignment-for. If the agent has zero delegations the handler returns a 404 instead of rendering — on the POST path as well as the GET, so a revoked delegation mid-journey 404s the submit (routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:29-32 and :38-42). If ?type is absent or is not CHEDPP the handler redirects to the 'What are you importing?' page (page-1) (:25-27; the query schema at :90-98 allows type=CHEDPP only). Corroborated by the workflow, which encodes a THREE-WAY branch after the Import Type page (ched-pp-workflows.ts:259-264): (a) config.organisationName is a non-empty string -> selectDifferentOrganisation() -> consignment-for THEN this page; (b) config.useOwnOrganisation is true -> selectOwnOrganisation() -> consignment-for ONLY, this page never renders; (c) neither set (the DEFAULT_CONFIG case: organisationName: '' at :136 and useOwnOrganisation: false at :137) -> NEITHER page renders and the journey goes Import Type straight to Country of Origin. Branch (c) is the standard non-DoA CHED-PP journey, so the default journey SKIPS this page entirely.
fields.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 — inputs id=organisation / organisation-2 / organisation-3, name=organisation, type=radio, values as above (all three GUIDs independently re-read from the DOM); accessible names from the a11y snapshot; conditionality from routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:24-35 and corroborated by /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:42-45 (config doc comments: organisationName "default: '' — skips org selection"; useOwnOrganisation "Whether to select the agent's own organisation on the org selection page (default: false)"), :136-137 (DEFAULT_CONFIG organisationName: '', useOwnOrganisation: false), :259-264 (the three-way branch that gates the page)

validationMessages.trigger: No organisation selected, an empty value, a non-string value, or a submitted organisation GUID that is not in the signed-in agent's delegation list. The SAME single message covers all four Joi error types (any.only, any.required, string.base, string.empty) — there is no distinct 'you picked one that isn't yours' message. On failure the handler re-renders this view with viewContext.errors set, so the error surfaces via the common/validationSummary partial and the organisationRadioGroup partial's own error rendering.
validationMessages.evidence: CONFIDENCE UPGRADED to 'legacy' — the string is now READ directly from the authorised IPAFFS source and independently re-verified this pass: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:157 `VALID_ORGANISATION_REQUIRED: 'Select a valid organisation'`, wired at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:12-17 (Joi.string().required().empty('').valid(...organisationIds) with ANY_VALID/ANY_REQUIRED/STRING_TYPE/STRING_EMPTY all mapped to that one constant). Per the run taxonomy a value read from authorised source is `legacy` (higher precedence than the previous `inferred`). Earlier note retained: CONFIDENCE CORRECTED from 'gap' to 'inferred' by re-derivation. A previous revision claimed this text was unverifiable because 'the legacy application source is out of scope' — that is wrong on two counts: (1) the run's own confidence taxonomy defines `inferred` as precisely 'deduced from test code, page objects, or legacy source but NOT observed in a trace', so legacy source is an admissible basis for `inferred`; (2) the spec already leans on legacy source (template + handler) for its other inferred claims, so excluding it only here was internally inconsistent. The message resolves to a literal with an exact citation: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:157 — `VALID_ORGANISATION_REQUIRED: 'Select a valid organisation'`, wired at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:12-17 (Joi.string().required().empty('').valid(...organisationIds) with all four error types mapped to that one constant); invoked from the route handler at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:44-50. STILL UNOBSERVED end-to-end: no trace renders it (all-pages.tsv:976 is the only corpus row for this page, actions 24-25, zero errors) and no test asserts it — the page object exposes no error/summary locator at all (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:1-42), so the suite is structurally incapable of asserting an error here. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN remains: 'Select a valid organisation' is the legacy string, not necessarily the right one — GDS style for a radio group would be 'Select the company this notification is for'. Confirm the rebuild's wording rather than porting this verbatim.

validationMessages.trigger: The hidden notification-type field is POSTed with anything other than CHEDPP. Not reachable through the UI — tamper/replay only — which is consistent with no test exercising it and no trace rendering it. Note the GET path treats the same condition differently: a missing/invalid ?type on GET redirects to the 'What are you importing?' page rather than showing this message (handler:25-27). Likely irrelevant to the rebuild if the rebuild does not carry a notification-type hidden field.
validationMessages.evidence: CONFIDENCE UPGRADED to 'legacy' — the message is a composed literal, now READ and independently re-verified this pass from authorised source. Built by invalidMessage(NOTIFICATION_TYPE): ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:19-21 (`invalidMessage = field => { return field + ' is not valid' }`) applied to ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/labels/en.js:69 (`NOTIFICATION_TYPE: 'Notification type'`) => 'Notification type is not valid'; wired at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:18-20 (only the ANY_VALID error type is mapped). Earlier note retained: CONFIDENCE CORRECTED from 'gap' to 'inferred'. The hidden input itself is CONFIRMED: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 (input type=hidden, name=notification-type, id=notification-type, value=CHEDPP). The RENDERING of the message is unobserved and unasserted. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: is a notification-type mismatch a real user-facing error, or purely a tamper guard that should 400?

validationMessages.message: Enter a valid save and continue button
validationMessages.trigger: TAMPER-ONLY. The validator ALWAYS composes SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA into this page's schema, so `save-and-continue` is an accepted (optional) payload key; it fails ANY_VALID only if POSTed with a value other than the expected button value. NOT reachable through the rendered UI — the create page's submit button carries NO name attribute, so this key is never actually submitted (see payloadContract). Never rendered in any trace and never asserted by any test. Captured here only because it is part of what this page's validator will accept; the rebuild should not inherit these optional save-action keys unless it renders named submit buttons.
validationMessages.evidence: Composed literal, READ from authorised source this pass: invalidEntryMessage(errorLabels.SAVE_AND_CONTINUE_BUTTON) where invalidEntryMessage = field => 'Enter a valid ' + field.toLowerCase() (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:23-25) applied to label 'Save and continue button' (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/labels/en.js:87) => 'Enter a valid save and continue button'; schema at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:14-21 (SAVE_AND_CONTINUE_SCHEMA, ANY_VALID mapped), folded into SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA :39-43, composed into this page's validator at validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:21 (...SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA).

validationMessages.message: Enter a valid save and return to hub button
validationMessages.trigger: TAMPER-ONLY, as save-and-continue. Accepted optional key from SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA; ANY_VALID error only. Never rendered or tested on this page; the create page submits no named save-action button.
validationMessages.evidence: invalidEntryMessage(errorLabels.SAVE_AND_RETURN_BUTTON) with label 'Save and return to hub button' (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/labels/en.js:88; invalidEntryMessage at validation/messages/en.js:23-25) => 'Enter a valid save and return to hub button'; SAVE_AND_RETURN_SCHEMA at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:22-29, folded into SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA :39-43, composed at validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:21.

validationMessages.message: Enter a valid save and review button
validationMessages.trigger: TAMPER-ONLY on the create page (accepted optional key from SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA, ANY_VALID error only). This is the key the 'Save and review' button (id=button-save-and-review) would submit in the amend/change flow when showSaveAndReviewButton is true — that button is exercised by a test (doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:95) but has NEVER been observed rendering, and this error message for it has never been asserted.
validationMessages.evidence: invalidEntryMessage(errorLabels.SAVE_AND_REVIEW_BUTTON) with label 'Save and review button' (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/labels/en.js:89; invalidEntryMessage at validation/messages/en.js:23-25) => 'Enter a valid save and review button'; SAVE_AND_REVIEW_SCHEMA at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:30-37, folded into SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA :39-43, composed at validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:21.

validationMessages.message: ETAG is not valid / ETAG must be a string / ETAG is missing
validationMessages.trigger: AMEND/EDIT FLOW ONLY. The validator folds ETAG_SCHEMA into this page's schema only when its requireEtag argument is set (the edit/amend route consignment_organisation_change.js, not the observed create flow — see openQuestions and the etag hidden-input note in structure). Three distinct messages: 'ETAG must be a string' (STRING_TYPE, non-string value); 'ETAG is not valid' (ruleset failure — etag not 16-18 chars); 'ETAG is missing' (ANY_REQUIRED — etag omitted). Verified absent on the create page (zero [name=etag] elements at action 24); never rendered in any trace and never asserted by any test. Likely irrelevant to the rebuild unless it carries an optimistic-concurrency etag.
validationMessages.evidence: ETAG_SCHEMA at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:45-52 — Joi.string().ruleset.min(16).max(18).rule({ message: invalidMessage(errorLabels.ETAG) }).required().messages({ [STRING_TYPE]: typeMessage(errorLabels.ETAG, 'string'), [ANY_REQUIRED]: ETAG_REQUIRED }); folded in at validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:24-29 when requireEtag. Strings resolve via label ETAG='ETAG' (validation/labels/en.js:52): invalidMessage(ETAG)='ETAG is not valid' (messages/en.js:19-21), typeMessage(ETAG,'string')='ETAG must be a string' (messages/en.js:27-29), ETAG_REQUIRED='ETAG is missing' (messages/en.js:77).

openQuestions: The evidence pointer was CORRECT — trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 24 is on this page (Page Title 'Which company is this notification for - Import and export applications - GOV.UK'), checking radio #organisation-2. The trace is tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-notification-csv.spec.ts:7, 'B2C Importer (Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification via CSV', signed in as users.b2cDoaAgent (Carol Clark) and passing organisationName: 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' — which matches the observed selection GUID eb0ca011-bc72-eb11-a812-000d3a274b26.

RESOLVED (was an open question in the previous revision; the corpus answers it). This page's list is DELEGATIONS ONLY — the agent's own organisation is not offered here. The page object hardcodes radioOwnOrganisation as 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:18-20), which is absent from this page's three options (Agency F, Organisation 1, Organisation 2). That is NOT staleness: the snapshot at action 22 shows the PREVIOUS page (consignment-for, H1 'Who are you creating this notification for?') rendering exactly two radios — 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' and 'A different organisation'. So Agency C is Carol Clark's own organisation, offered only on consignment-for, and it correctly never appears here. Independently corroborated by doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:109, which asserts the own-org notification's organisation name is 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C'. FURTHER corroborated by the workflow's own-org branch: useOwnOrganisation: true calls selectOwnOrganisation(), which is a single check+click on consignment-for and never touches this page (ched-pp-workflows.ts:262-263; exercised by doa-agent-access.spec.ts:71, 'Carol Clark (agent) submits for own org (Plant Agency C) → Isabel Irwin (Plant Org 1 member) cannot see it'). CONSEQUENCE FOR THE REBUILD: a single combined radio group must list the own org ALONGSIDE the delegations — the two lists are disjoint today and both are needed.

STILL OPEN — is the agent's own organisation selectable on this page in the AMEND flow? tests/auth/DoA/doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:93-95 clicks btnAmend, then linkChangeOrganisation (#organisation-change-link on the review page — ReviewNotificationPage.ts:125-127), then calls selectOwnOrganisationAndSaveAndReview() — i.e. it checks radio 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' and clicks 'Save and review', in ONE page step with no intermediate 'A different organisation' hop. If #organisation-change-link lands on THIS page, then in amend mode the page lists the own organisation too and behaves differently from create mode (and would be the only place 'Save and review' renders). If it lands on consignment-for, it does not. The amend variant of this page appears in ZERO traces, so this cannot be resolved from the corpus. Note the QA repo cannot disambiguate it either, because one page object spans both pages. A human should confirm which page #organisation-change-link targets.

The H1 has NO question mark — verbatim 'Which company is this notification for'. The PREVIOUS page in the same flow DOES punctuate its question ('Who are you creating this notification for?'), so this is an inconsistency within one journey, not a house style. GDS copy defect — the rebuild should fix it, but note the page-name change.

The radios have NO fieldset and NO legend (verified: fieldsets=0, legends=0 in main). This is the single most important finding on the page — do not copy it. AGGRAVATED, and now with a root cause: the CHED-PP accessibility suite pins itself to a NON-DoA user (ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:16, storageState: AUTH_FILES.b2cNotifier) and therefore CANNOT reach this page at all — it drives that user from the Import Type Page at :23 straight to the Country of Origin Page at :32. axe has never scanned this markup and cannot until the suite adds a DoA-agent pass. The rebuild must add a DoA-agent a11y scan covering the organisation page.

The 8-organisation radios-to-select switch is entirely untraced AND structurally untestable by the current suite — OrganisationSelectionPage exposes radio locators only, with no select/dropdown getter (contrast CountryOfOriginPage.dropdownCountryOfOrigin, used at ched-pp-workflows.ts:268). So the select variant's rendering, its 'Select organisation' top option, its visually-hidden label and its error display are unverified by BOTH sources, and any agent with 8+ delegations would break the whole DoA suite. Decide the rebuild's control strategy deliberately rather than porting the threshold.

The overdue-debt path is entirely untraced and also untested: the shouldShowOverdueDebt warning partial, the disabled options and the 'This option is not available' hint/suffix never rendered, and no fixture in the QA repo creates an overdue-debt organisation. COPY GAP NOW CLOSED (legacy): the overdueDebtWarningDoA partial HAS been read (overdueDebtWarningDoA.html:1-31) — on this page it is a collapsible govuk-details ('Why some options are not available') carrying 'There is a problem', 'You cannot submit a CHEDPP import notification on behalf of {org names}.' and 'Please ask {org names} to contact us on 01633 631800 to discuss the reactivation of their account.'; on the review page the same data instead drives a govuk-error-summary. So it is NOT a govuk-warning-text banner despite the structure/component labels a previous revision inferred from the partial name. NEWLY ESTABLISHED and worth a human answer: an overdue-debt org has a THIRD, non-obvious side effect — because the client script skips the autocomplete enhancement whenever any option is disabled, an 8+-delegation agent with one overdue debtor silently loses the autocomplete and gets a bare select. So the overdue-debt flag changes the CONTROL, not just the option. What is the actual business rule for blocking an overdue debtor, and must the rebuild keep it?

The 'Save and review' button variant (id=button-save-and-review, shown when showSaveAndReviewButton is true) is untraced but IS exercised by a test — doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:95 via OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:31-34. It is the amend/change flow returning to the review page. Its existence is inferred-with-citation; its RENDERING is unobserved, and the only button in main at action 24 is 'Save and continue'. It has therefore been removed from the page's secondaryActions.

The etag hidden field only renders when the view context carries an etag, which points at an edit/amend route (/protected/notifications/{referenceNumber}/consignment-organisation, routes.js:1128-1134, handled by routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation_change.js — the fourth same-named file, see citationNote). Verified absent in the observed create flow (zero [name=etag] elements at action 24). The validator folds in ETAG_SCHEMA only when its requireEtag argument is set (validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:24-29; common_schemas.js:45-49 — etag is required, 16-18 chars), which corroborates the create/amend split. That change variant of this page was not traced (all-pages.tsv:976 is the only row for this page, the create-flow one) and may differ.

The organisationRadioGroup partial ({{> data/organisationRadioGroup}}) could not be found anywhere in the ipaffs checkout outside node_modules — it appears to come from a shared component library. The rendered HTML in the trace is the ground truth used here; the partial's own conditional logic (how it renders errors, hints and disabled items) is unverified. This is now the single largest remaining source hole on the page: it is the partial that decides the no-fieldset markup, the id scheme (including the `organisation` id collision that breaks the client script), and the unobserved error/disabled rendering. A human with access to the component library should read it.

NEW FINDING — the page ships a page-specific JS bundle that is BROKEN on the only path anyone has ever traced. assets/javascripts/consignment_organisation.js enhances $('#organisation') into an accessible autocomplete, but on the radio path `organisation` is the FIRST RADIO's id, so it hands a radio to enhanceSelectElement() and throws 'Array.prototype.filter called on null or undefined' — visible in THIS trace's console at 0:17.553, stack naming consignment_organisation.js:10:30, timed between action 24 (0:17.395) and action 25 (0:17.673). The exception is swallowed by jQuery's ready wrapper and the plain-HTML radios still submit, so the page 'works' by luck. Two consequences: (a) the rebuild must not port this script; (b) it is evidence that the trace corpus's 'Errors: 0' at TEST level does not mean the page is clean at RUNTIME — worth re-checking other pages' consoles for the same class of silent breakage.

Options are NOT reference data and NOT a fixed list — they are the agent's organisations, fetched per-request from the customer service (customerService.getDelegations; routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:85-88, on BOTH the GET and the POST path, so the allowed-value set is re-fetched before every validation). Corroborated by source 2: there is no organisation entry in types/ (no organisation.ts alongside country.ts, commodity-code.ts, package-type.ts etc.), and the tests pass organisation names as free strings through ChedPPNotificationConfig.organisationName (ched-pp-workflows.ts:43) rather than from a domain enum. They are sorted alphabetically, case-insensitively, by the composed text — `_.orderBy(delegationOptions, [org => org.text.toLowerCase()], ['asc'])` at routes/handlers/importer/consignment_organisation.js:60 — which matches the observed order (Agency F, Organisation 1, Organisation 2). Note the sort key is the COMPOSED text (name + ', ' + address), not the name alone, so two orgs with the same name would be ordered by address. The rebuild needs an equivalent per-user organisations lookup.

The radio value is the organisation GUID, and on success the handler redirects to consignment-origin carrying that GUID as the orgId query param. CONFIRMED at the network level: POST /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/consignment-organisation?type=CHEDPP -> 302 with Location: /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/consignment-origin?type=CHEDPP&orgId=eb0ca011-bc72-eb11-a812-000d3a274b26 (trace request 911). Note the chosen organisation is carried forward in the URL rather than in session state — a rebuild decision point.

REVISED — a previous revision called this 'the biggest honest hole in the page' and marked both validation messages 'gap' on the stated ground that 'the legacy application source is out of scope for requirements'. That ground was wrong: the run's taxonomy explicitly admits legacy source as a basis for `inferred`, and the spec relied on legacy source (template + handler) for its other inferred claims regardless. Both messages have now been resolved from source and re-tagged `inferred` — 'Select a valid organisation' (validation/messages/en.js:157) and 'Notification type is not valid' (invalidMessage + labels/en.js:69). The genuine, narrower hole is UNCHANGED and still needs a human: no error state for this page exists in any of the 65 CHED-PP traces and no test asserts one, so the RENDERING of these errors (does the summary link to the radio group? what is the field-level anchor, given there is no fieldset to anchor to?) is unverified. The QA suite could not assert an error even if one fired: OrganisationSelectionPage has no error-summary or field-error locator.

Only ONE organisation name is ever selected by any test on this page — 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' (doa-agent-access.spec.ts:15,42; doa-importer-access.spec.ts:15,42; doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:44,76,120; ched-pp-notification-csv.spec.ts:14; verified exhaustively by grep for organisationName across tests/ and workflows/). No test ever picks the second or third radio, so nothing verifies that selecting a DIFFERENT organisation carries the right GUID through to the notification. The rebuild's test suite should cover at least two distinct organisations.

Downstream consequence of this page, worth capturing as a requirement rather than a page detail: selecting an organisation here auto-populates the contact details AND the importer from that organisation. doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:113-138 ('Carol Clark (agent) creates for Plant Org 1 → contact and importer auto-populated from Plant Org 1') asserts contact name 'Isabel Irwin', contact email 'ipaffs-plant-org-1-isabel@mailinator.com', telephone '0123456789', and importer 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' / 'KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6, UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT' — i.e. the contact is the DELEGATED ORG's contact person, not the acting agent (Carol Clark). The choice made here is not just a label — it seeds later pages.

Second downstream consequence, asserted by source 2 and not visible on the page itself: the organisation chosen here determines who can SEE the resulting notification. doa-agent-access.spec.ts:60-86 asserts that when Carol Clark creates for her OWN org (useOwnOrganisation: true), Isabel Irwin — a member of Plant Org 1 — cannot find the notification on her dashboard; whereas the delegated-org route (organisationName: 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1') makes it visible to that org's members (doa-importer-access.spec.ts:15,42). So this radio is an ACCESS-CONTROL decision, not just a data field. The rebuild must treat the selected organisation as the notification's owning tenant and carry that into its authorisation model.
